title: Aiphanes horrida
taxonomy: Aiphanes horrida is a species of palm tree, and as such, it belongs to the family Arecaceae. This family encompasses a wide range of palm species, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. Aiphanes horrida is specifically classified under the genus Aiphanes, which includes several species of palms found in Central and South America. The classification of Aiphanes horrida is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf and inflorescence structure. By classifying Aiphanes horrida within the Arecaceae family, botanists can better understand its evolutionary relationships with other palm species.

intro: Aiphanes horrida is a species of palm tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Arecaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and pain, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
